message:
feat(channel): add Receiver::close() and fix actor mailbox drain to guarantee no silent drops

Two coupled changes that close a correctness gap in actor shutdown sequencing:

Receiver::close() (src/channel/mpsc.rs):
- New method that seals the channel to prevent further sends while leaving
  existing queued messages available for receiving
- Sets receiver_dropped flag, wakes all pending senders so they get
  Disconnected errors immediately
- Required by the actor drain fix below

Actor mailbox drain fix (src/actor.rs):
- Replace capacity-based drain limit with explicit mailbox.close() call
  before draining
- Previous approach: drained up to capacity messages, then stopped — this
  could silently drop messages committed to the mailbox between the capacity
  snapshot and the drain loop finishing, violating the "no message silently
  dropped" guarantee
- New approach: seal the mailbox first (preventing new reservations/commits),
  then drain all remaining messages unconditionally before running on_stop
- This ensures the two-phase mailbox guarantee holds: every message that was
  successfully committed is handled before the actor stops

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
